root_text.an10.29:1.1: “Yāvatā, bhikkhave, kāsikosalā, yāvatā rañño pasenadissa kosalassa vijitaṁ, rājā tattha pasenadi kosalo aggamakkhāyati.
root_text.an10.29:1.2: Raññopi kho, bhikkhave, pasenadissa kosalassa attheva aññathattaṁ atthi vipariṇāmo.
root_text.an10.29:1.3: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.an10.29:1.4: Tasmiṁ nibbindanto agge virajjati, pageva hīnasmiṁ.
root_text.an10.29:2.1: Yāvatā, bhikkhave, candimasūriyā pariharanti disā bhanti virocamānā, tāva sahassadhā loko.
root_text.an10.29:2.2: Tasmiṁ sahassadhā loke sahassaṁ candānaṁ sahassaṁ sūriyānaṁ sahassaṁ sinerupabbatarājānaṁ sahassaṁ jambudīpānaṁ sahassaṁ aparagoyānānaṁ sahassaṁ uttarakurūnaṁ sahassaṁ pubbavidehānaṁ cattāri mahāsamuddasahassāni cattāri mahārājasahassāni sahassaṁ cātumahārājikānaṁ sahassaṁ tāvatiṁsānaṁ sahassaṁ yāmānaṁ sahassaṁ tusitānaṁ sahassaṁ nimmānaratīnaṁ sahassaṁ paranimmitavasavattīnaṁ sahassaṁ brahmalokānaṁ.
root_text.an10.29:2.3: Yāvatā, bhikkhave, sahassī lokadhātu, mahābrahmā tattha aggamakkhāyati.
root_text.an10.29:2.4: Mahābrahmunopi kho, bhikkhave, attheva aññathattaṁ atthi vipariṇāmo.
root_text.an10.29:2.5: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.an10.29:2.6: Tasmiṁ nibbindanto agge virajjati, pageva hīnasmiṁ.
root_text.an10.29:3.1: Hoti so, bhikkhave, samayo yaṁ ayaṁ loko saṁvaṭṭati.
root_text.an10.29:3.2: Saṁvaṭṭamāne, bhikkhave, loke yebhuyyena sattā ābhassarasaṁvattanikā bhavanti.
root_text.an10.29:3.3: Te tattha honti manomayā pītibhakkhā sayaṁpabhā antalikkhecarā subhaṭṭhāyino ciraṁ dīghamaddhānaṁ tiṭṭhanti.
root_text.an10.29:3.4: Saṁvaṭṭamāne, bhikkhave, loke ābhassarā devā aggamakkhāyanti.
root_text.an10.29:3.5: Ābhassarānampi kho, bhikkhave, devānaṁ attheva aññathattaṁ atthi vipariṇāmo.
root_text.an10.29:3.6: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.an10.29:3.7: Tasmiṁ nibbindanto agge virajjati, pageva hīnasmiṁ.
root_text.an10.29:4.3: Pathavīkasiṇameko sañjānāti uddhaṁ adho tiriyaṁ advayaṁ appamāṇaṁ;
root_text.an10.29:4.12: viññāṇakasiṇameko sañjānāti uddhaṁ adho tiriyaṁ advayaṁ appamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an10.29:5.1: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dasannaṁ kasiṇāyatanānaṁ yadidaṁ viññāṇakasiṇaṁ eko sañjānāti uddhaṁ adho tiriyaṁ advayaṁ appamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an10.29:5.3: Evaṁsaññīnampi kho, bhikkhave, sattānaṁ attheva aññathattaṁ atthi vipariṇāmo.
root_text.an10.29:5.4: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.an10.29:5.5: Tasmiṁ nibbindanto agge virajjati, pageva hīnasmiṁ.
root_text.an10.29:6.3: Ajjhattaṁ rūpasaññī eko bahiddhā rūpāni passati parittāni suvaṇṇadubbaṇṇāni;
root_text.an10.29:7.1: Ajjhattaṁ rūpasaññī eko bahiddhā rūpāni passati appamāṇāni suvaṇṇadubbaṇṇāni;
root_text.an10.29:8.1: Ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī eko bahiddhā rūpāni passati parittāni suvaṇṇadubbaṇṇāni;
root_text.an10.29:9.1: Ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī eko bahiddhā rūpāni passati appamāṇāni suvaṇṇadubbaṇṇāni;
root_text.an10.29:10.1: Ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī eko bahiddhā rūpāni passati nīlāni nīlavaṇṇāni nīlanidassanāni nīlanibhāsāni.
root_text.an10.29:10.2: Seyyathāpi nāma umāpupphaṁ nīlaṁ nīlavaṇṇaṁ nīlanidassanaṁ nīlanibhāsaṁ, seyyathā vā pana taṁ vatthaṁ bārāṇaseyyakaṁ ubhatobhāgavimaṭṭhaṁ nīlaṁ nīlavaṇṇaṁ nīlanidassanaṁ nīlanibhāsaṁ;
root_text.an10.29:10.3: evamevaṁ ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī eko bahiddhā rūpāni passati nīlāni nīlavaṇṇāni nīlanidassanāni nīlanibhāsāni;
root_text.an10.29:11.1: Ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī eko bahiddhā rūpāni passati pītāni pītavaṇṇāni pītanidassanāni pītanibhāsāni.
root_text.an10.29:11.2: Seyyathāpi nāma kaṇikārapupphaṁ pītaṁ pītavaṇṇaṁ pītanidassanaṁ pītanibhāsaṁ, seyyathā vā pana taṁ vatthaṁ bārāṇaseyyakaṁ ubhatobhāgavimaṭṭhaṁ pītaṁ pītavaṇṇaṁ pītanidassanaṁ pītanibhāsaṁ;
root_text.an10.29:11.3: evamevaṁ ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī eko bahiddhā rūpāni passati pītāni pītavaṇṇāni pītanidassanāni pītanibhāsāni;
root_text.an10.29:12.1: Ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī eko bahiddhā rūpāni passati lohitakāni lohitakavaṇṇāni lohitakanidassanāni lohitakanibhāsāni.
root_text.an10.29:12.2: Seyyathāpi nāma bandhujīvakapupphaṁ lohitakaṁ lohitakavaṇṇaṁ lohitakanidassanaṁ lohitakanibhāsaṁ, seyyathā vā pana taṁ vatthaṁ bārāṇaseyyakaṁ ubhatobhāgavimaṭṭhaṁ lohitakaṁ lohitakavaṇṇaṁ lohitakanidassanaṁ lohitakanibhāsaṁ;
root_text.an10.29:12.3: evamevaṁ ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī eko bahiddhā rūpāni passati lohitakāni lohitakavaṇṇāni lohitakanidassanāni lohitakanibhāsāni;
root_text.an10.29:13.1: Ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī eko bahiddhā rūpāni passati odātāni odātavaṇṇāni odātanidassanāni odātanibhāsāni.
root_text.an10.29:13.2: Seyyathāpi nāma osadhitārakā odātā odātavaṇṇā odātanidassanā odātanibhāsā, seyyathā vā pana taṁ vatthaṁ bārāṇaseyyakaṁ ubhatobhāgavimaṭṭhaṁ odātaṁ odātavaṇṇaṁ odātanidassanaṁ odātanibhāsaṁ;
root_text.an10.29:13.3: evamevaṁ ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī eko bahiddhā rūpāni passati odātāni odātavaṇṇāni odātanidassanāni odātanibhāsāni;
root_text.an10.29:14.1: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ aṭṭhannaṁ abhibhāyatanānaṁ yadidaṁ ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī eko bahiddhā rūpāni passati odātāni odātavaṇṇāni odātanidassanāni odātanibhāsāni;
root_text.an10.29:14.4: Evaṁsaññīnampi kho, bhikkhave, sattānaṁ attheva aññathattaṁ atthi vipariṇāmo.
root_text.an10.29:14.5: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.an10.29:14.6: Tasmiṁ nibbindanto agge virajjati, pageva hīnasmiṁ.
root_text.an10.29:16.1: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imāsaṁ catunnaṁ paṭipadānaṁ yadidaṁ sukhā paṭipadā khippābhiññā.
root_text.an10.29:16.3: Evaṁpaṭipannānampi kho, bhikkhave, sattānaṁ attheva aññathattaṁ atthi vipariṇāmo.
root_text.an10.29:16.4: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.an10.29:16.5: Tasmiṁ nibbindanto agge virajjati, pageva hīnasmiṁ.
root_text.an10.29:18.1: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imāsaṁ catunnaṁ saññānaṁ yadidaṁ ‘natthi kiñcī’ti ākiñcaññāyatanameko sañjānāti.
root_text.an10.29:18.3: Evaṁsaññīnampi kho, bhikkhave, sattānaṁ attheva aññathattaṁ atthi vipariṇāmo.
root_text.an10.29:18.4: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.an10.29:18.5: Tasmiṁ nibbindanto agge virajjati, pageva hīnasmiṁ.
root_text.an10.29:19.1: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, bāhirakānaṁ diṭṭhigatānaṁ yadidaṁ ‘no cassaṁ, no ca me siyā, na bhavissāmi, na me bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an10.29:19.3: ‘yā cāyaṁ bhave appaṭikulyatā, sā cassa na bhavissati;
root_text.an10.29:19.4: yā cāyaṁ bhavanirodhe pāṭikulyatā, sā cassa na bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an10.29:19.6: Evaṁdiṭṭhīnampi kho, bhikkhave, sattānaṁ attheva aññathattaṁ atthi vipariṇāmo.
root_text.an10.29:19.7: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.an10.29:19.8: Tasmiṁ nibbindanto agge virajjati, pageva hīnasmiṁ.
root_text.an10.29:20.1: Santi, bhikkhave, eke samaṇabrāhmaṇā paramatthavisuddhiṁ paññāpenti.
root_text.an10.29:20.2: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, paramatthavisuddhiṁ paññapentānaṁ yadidaṁ sabbaso ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an10.29:20.3: Te tadabhiññāya tassa sacchikiriyāya dhammaṁ desenti.
root_text.an10.29:20.5: Evaṁvādīnampi kho, bhikkhave, sattānaṁ attheva aññathattaṁ atthi vipariṇāmo.
root_text.an10.29:20.6: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.an10.29:20.7: Tasmiṁ nibbindanto agge virajjati, pageva hīnasmiṁ.
root_text.an10.29:21.1: Santi, bhikkhave, eke samaṇabrāhmaṇā paramadiṭṭhadhammanibbānaṁ paññāpenti.
root_text.an10.29:21.2: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, paramadiṭṭhadhammanibbānaṁ paññapentānaṁ yadidaṁ channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ viditvā anupādā vimokkho.
root_text.an10.29:21.3: Evaṁvādiṁ kho maṁ, bhikkhave, evamakkhāyiṁ eke samaṇabrāhmaṇā asatā tucchā musā abhūtena abbhācikkhanti:
root_text.an10.29:21.4: ‘samaṇo gotamo na kāmānaṁ pariññaṁ paññāpeti, na rūpānaṁ pariññaṁ paññāpeti, na vedanānaṁ pariññaṁ paññāpetī’ti.
root_text.an10.29:21.5: Kāmānañcāhaṁ, bhikkhave, pariññaṁ paññāpemi, rūpānañca pariññaṁ paññāpemi, vedanānañca pariññaṁ paññāpemi, diṭṭheva dhamme nicchāto nibbuto sītibhūto anupādā parinibbānaṁ paññāpemī”ti.

translation_text.an10.29:1.1: “As far as Kāsi and Kosala extend, and as far as the dominion of King Pasenadi of Kosala extends, King Pasenadi is said to be the foremost.
translation_text.an10.29:1.2: But even King Pasenadi decays and perishes.
translation_text.an10.29:1.3: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with it.
translation_text.an10.29:1.4: Their desire fades away even for the foremost, let alone the inferior.
translation_text.an10.29:2.1: A galaxy extends a thousand times as far as the moon and sun revolve and the shining ones light up the quarters.
translation_text.an10.29:2.2: In that galaxy there are a thousand moons, a thousand suns, a thousand Sinerus king of mountains, a thousand Black Plum Tree Lands, a thousand lands west of Godāvarī, a thousand lands north of Kuru, a thousand lands east of Videha, four thousand oceans, four thousand great kings, a thousand realms of the gods of the four great kings, a thousand realms of the gods of the thirty-three, of the gods of Yama, of the joyful gods, of the gods who love to create, of the gods who control what is created by others, and a thousand realms of divinity.
translation_text.an10.29:2.3: As far as the galaxy extends, the Great Divinity is said to be the foremost.
translation_text.an10.29:2.4: But even the Great Divinity decays and perishes.
translation_text.an10.29:2.5: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with it.
translation_text.an10.29:2.6: Their desire fades away even for the foremost, let alone the inferior.
translation_text.an10.29:3.1: There comes a time when this cosmos contracts.
translation_text.an10.29:3.2: As it contracts, most sentient beings migrate to the realm of streaming radiance.
translation_text.an10.29:3.3: There they are mind-made, feeding on rapture, self-luminous, wandering in midair, steadily glorious, and they remain like that for a very long time.
translation_text.an10.29:3.4: When the cosmos is contracting, the gods of streaming radiance are said to be the foremost.
translation_text.an10.29:3.5: But even the gods of streaming radiance decay and perish.
translation_text.an10.29:3.6: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with it.
translation_text.an10.29:3.7: Their desire fades away even for the foremost, let alone the inferior.
translation_text.an10.29:4.1: There are these ten universal dimensions of meditation.
translation_text.an10.29:4.3: Someone perceives the meditation on universal earth above, below, across, undivided and limitless.
translation_text.an10.29:4.4: They perceive the meditation on universal water …
translation_text.an10.29:4.12: They perceive the meditation on universal consciousness above, below, across, undivided and limitless.
translation_text.an10.29:4.13: These are the ten universal dimensions of meditation.
translation_text.an10.29:5.1: The best of these ten universal dimensions of meditation is when someone perceives the meditation on universal consciousness above, below, across, undivided and limitless.
translation_text.an10.29:5.2: Some sentient beings perceive like this.
translation_text.an10.29:5.3: But even the sentient beings who perceive like this decay and perish.
translation_text.an10.29:5.4: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with it.
translation_text.an10.29:5.5: Their desire fades away even for the foremost, let alone the inferior.
translation_text.an10.29:6.1: There are these eight dimensions of mastery.
translation_text.an10.29:6.3: Perceiving form internally, someone sees forms externally, limited, both pretty and ugly.
translation_text.an10.29:6.4: Mastering them, they perceive: ‘I know and see.’
translation_text.an10.29:6.5: This is the first dimension of mastery.
translation_text.an10.29:7.1: Perceiving form internally, someone sees forms externally, limitless, both pretty and ugly.
translation_text.an10.29:7.2: Mastering them, they perceive: ‘I know and see.’
translation_text.an10.29:7.3: This is the second dimension of mastery.
translation_text.an10.29:8.1: Not perceiving form internally, someone sees forms externally, limited, both pretty and ugly.
translation_text.an10.29:8.2: Mastering them, they perceive: ‘I know and see.’
translation_text.an10.29:8.3: This is the third dimension of mastery.
translation_text.an10.29:9.1: Not perceiving form internally, someone sees forms externally, limitless, both pretty and ugly.
translation_text.an10.29:9.2: Mastering them, they perceive: ‘I know and see.’
translation_text.an10.29:9.3: This is the fourth dimension of mastery.
translation_text.an10.29:10.1: Not perceiving form internally, someone sees forms externally, blue, with blue color and blue appearance.
translation_text.an10.29:10.2: They’re like a flax flower that’s blue, with blue color and blue appearance. Or a cloth from Varanasi that’s smoothed on both sides, blue, with blue color and blue appearance.
translation_text.an10.29:10.3: In the same way, not perceiving form internally, someone sees forms externally, blue, with blue color and blue appearance.
translation_text.an10.29:10.4: Mastering them, they perceive: ‘I know and see.’
translation_text.an10.29:10.5: This is the fifth dimension of mastery.
translation_text.an10.29:11.1: Not perceiving form internally, someone sees forms externally, yellow, with yellow color and yellow appearance.
translation_text.an10.29:11.2: They’re like a champak flower that’s yellow, with yellow color and yellow appearance. Or a cloth from Varanasi that’s smoothed on both sides, yellow, with yellow color and yellow appearance.
translation_text.an10.29:11.3: In the same way, not perceiving form internally, someone sees forms externally, yellow, with yellow color and yellow appearance.
translation_text.an10.29:11.4: Mastering them, they perceive: ‘I know and see.’
translation_text.an10.29:11.5: This is the sixth dimension of mastery.
translation_text.an10.29:12.1: Not perceiving form internally, someone sees forms externally, red, with red color and red appearance.
translation_text.an10.29:12.2: They’re like a scarlet mallow flower that’s red, with red color and red appearance. Or a cloth from Varanasi that’s smoothed on both sides, red, with red color and red appearance.
translation_text.an10.29:12.3: In the same way, not perceiving form internally, someone sees forms externally, red, with red color and red appearance.
translation_text.an10.29:12.4: Mastering them, they perceive: ‘I know and see.’
translation_text.an10.29:12.5: This is the seventh dimension of mastery.
translation_text.an10.29:13.1: Not perceiving form internally, someone sees forms externally, white, with white color and white appearance.
translation_text.an10.29:13.2: They’re like the morning star that’s white, with white color and white appearance. Or a cloth from Varanasi that’s smoothed on both sides, white, with white color and white appearance.
translation_text.an10.29:13.3: In the same way, not perceiving form internally, someone sees forms externally, white, with white color and white appearance.
translation_text.an10.29:13.4: Mastering them, they perceive: ‘I know and see.’
translation_text.an10.29:13.5: This is the eighth dimension of mastery.
translation_text.an10.29:13.6: These are the eight dimensions of mastery.
translation_text.an10.29:14.1: The best of these dimensions of mastery is when someone, not perceiving form internally, sees forms externally, white, with white color and white appearance.
translation_text.an10.29:14.2: Mastering them, they perceive: ‘I know and see.’
translation_text.an10.29:14.3: Some sentient beings perceive like this.
translation_text.an10.29:14.4: But even the sentient beings who perceive like this decay and perish.
translation_text.an10.29:14.5: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with it.
translation_text.an10.29:14.6: Their desire fades away even for the foremost, let alone the inferior.
translation_text.an10.29:15.1: There are four ways of practice.
translation_text.an10.29:15.5: pleasant practice with slow insight, and
translation_text.an10.29:15.7: These are the four ways of practice.
translation_text.an10.29:16.1: The best of these four ways of practice is the pleasant practice with swift insight.
translation_text.an10.29:16.2: Some sentient beings practice like this.
translation_text.an10.29:16.3: But even the sentient beings who practice like this decay and perish.
translation_text.an10.29:16.4: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with it.
translation_text.an10.29:16.5: Their desire fades away even for the foremost, let alone the inferior.
translation_text.an10.29:17.6: One individual, aware that ‘there is nothing at all’, perceives the dimension of nothingness.
translation_text.an10.29:18.1: The best of these four perceptions is when someone, aware that ‘there is nothing at all’, perceives the dimension of nothingness.
translation_text.an10.29:18.2: Some sentient beings perceive like this.
translation_text.an10.29:18.3: But even the sentient beings who perceive like this decay and perish.
translation_text.an10.29:18.4: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with it.
translation_text.an10.29:18.5: Their desire fades away even for the foremost, let alone the inferior.
translation_text.an10.29:19.1: This is the best of the convictions of outsiders, that is: ‘I might not be, and it might not be mine. I will not be, and it will not be mine.’
translation_text.an10.29:19.2: When someone has such a view, you can expect
translation_text.an10.29:19.3: that they will not be attracted to continued existence,
translation_text.an10.29:19.4: and they will not be repulsed by the cessation of continued existence.
translation_text.an10.29:19.5: Some sentient beings have such a view.
translation_text.an10.29:19.6: But even the sentient beings who have views like this decay and perish.
translation_text.an10.29:19.7: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with it.
translation_text.an10.29:19.8: Their desire fades away even for the foremost, let alone the inferior.
translation_text.an10.29:20.1: There are some ascetics and brahmins who advocate the ultimate purity of the spirit.
translation_text.an10.29:20.2: This is the best of the advocates of the ultimate purity of the spirit, that is, when someone, going totally beyond the dimension of nothingness, enters and remains in the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception.
translation_text.an10.29:20.3: They teach Dhamma in order to directly know and realize this.
translation_text.an10.29:20.4: Some sentient beings have such a doctrine.
translation_text.an10.29:20.5: But even the sentient beings who have such a doctrine decay and perish.
translation_text.an10.29:20.6: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with it.
translation_text.an10.29:20.7: Their desire fades away even for the foremost, let alone the inferior.
translation_text.an10.29:21.1: There are some ascetics and brahmins who advocate ultimate extinguishment in this very life.
translation_text.an10.29:21.2: This is the best of those who advocate extinguishment in this very life, that is, liberation by not grasping after truly understanding the origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape of the six fields of contact.
translation_text.an10.29:21.3: Though I state and assert this, certain ascetics and brahmins misrepresent me with the incorrect, hollow, false, untruthful claim:
translation_text.an10.29:21.4: ‘The ascetic Gotama doesn’t advocate the complete understanding of sensual pleasures, forms, or feelings.’
translation_text.an10.29:21.5: But I do advocate the complete understanding of sensual pleasures, forms, and feelings. And I advocate full extinguishment by not grasping in this very life, wishless, quenched, and cooled.”
